props.description: The size of the Combobox.
The prefix to display in the ComboBox. A non-interactive element that appears before the input.
The contents of the collection.
Direction the menu will render relative to the ComboBox.
Alignment of the menu relative to the input target.
Width of the menu. By default, matches width of the trigger. Note that the minimum width of the dropdown is always equal to the trigger's width.
The current loading state of the ComboBox. Determines whether or not the progress circle should be shown.
The filter function used to determine if a option should be included in the combo box list.
Whether the text or key of the selected item is submitted as part of an HTML form. When `allowsCustomValue` is `true`, this option does not apply and the text is always submitted.
Whether the combo box allows the menu to be open when the collection is empty.
Spectrum-defined styles, returned by the `style()` macro.
How multiple selection should behave in the collection.
The drag and drop hooks returned by `useDragAndDrop` used to enable drag and drop behavior for the ListBox.
Provides content to display when there are no items in the list.
Whether the items are arranged in a stack or grid.
The primary orientation of the items. Usually this is the direction that the collection scrolls.

The name of the component that triggered the popover. This is reflected on the element as the `data-trigger` attribute, and can be used to provide specific styles for the popover depending on which element triggered it.

The ref for the element which the popover positions itself with respect to. When used within a trigger component such as DialogTrigger, MenuTrigger, Select, etc., this is set automatically. It is only required when used standalone.
Whether the popover is currently performing an entry animation.
Whether the popover is currently performing an exit animation.
The container element in which the overlay portal will be placed. This may have unknown behavior depending on where it is portalled to.
The additional offset applied along the main axis between the element and its anchor element.
Temporary text that occupies the text input when it is empty. See [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/placeholder).
